title: Behavioral Signals - When Timing is Everything

questions.question: What is the key difference between Static Data and Dynamic Signals?

options.text: Static Data changes weekly; Dynamic Signals never change.
Static Data tells you 'who' they are; Dynamic Signals tell you 'what state' they are in (timing).
Static Data is for B2B; Dynamic Signals are for B2C.
There is no difference.

questions.explanation: Static data (size, location) confirms fit. Dynamic signals (hiring, funding) confirm timing/urgency.

questions.question: Why is a 'New Executive' (Job Change < 90 days) such a powerful sales signal?

options.text: They don't know how to say 'no' yet.
They have a mandate for change, a budget, and no loyalty to legacy tools.
They usually have a smaller budget than the previous person.
They are lonely and want to talk to salespeople.

questions.explanation: New leaders are hired to fix problems. They are 10x more likely to buy new solutions to make their mark.

questions.question: If a company posts a job description for a specific role (e.g., 'React Developer'), what does this signal?

options.text: They are firing people.
They have an internal gap or pain point that they are trying to solve with a human.
They are too busy to talk to you.
They simply like posting jobs.

questions.explanation: A job post is a 'public confession of pain.' If they are hiring for X, they are struggling with X.

questions.question: What is the 'Tech Install' signal?

options.text: When a company buys new computers.
When the wifi goes down.
When a company adds or removes a specific software tool from their website/stack.
When a developer writes code.

questions.explanation: Monitoring tools like BuiltWith can show when a company adds a competitor or complementary tool, signalling intent.

questions.question: Which of these is the BEST way to use funding news (Series A raise)?

options.text: Ask them for a donation.
Congratulate them and ask for 15 minutes of their time.
Ignore the money and pitch the 'Pressure to Grow' that comes with it.
Assume they are too rich to care about saving money.

questions.explanation: Funding creates pressure. Investors demand growth. Pitch how you help them achieve that growth.

questions.question: List 3 specific behavioral signals or triggers that indicate a company is ready to buy your solution. How would you detect each signal?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the behavioral triggers. Check for: (1) specificity of signals, (2) detectability, (3) correlation to buying intent. Suggest additional signals or detection methods.
